game_name: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: The Hyperstone Heist

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundational mechanics with basic street enemies
Dedicated button assignment; core evasion technique
Limited compared to Turtles in Time (cannot throw toward screen)
Pattern-based combat; escalating difficulty through level progression
Faster-moving gameplay than arcade predecessor; requires reactive play
Themed level geometry; varied spatial layouts
Story context: Manhattan shrinking → pursuit through varied environments → final confrontation

typical_tension_triggers: Boss encounters (Leatherhead, Rocksteady, Tatsu, Baxter Stockman, Krang, Super Shredder)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Blast-a-minute arcade pacing with aggressive encounters and fast-moving gameplay; sustained combat pressure throughout each level with boss climax

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible, described as too easy by multiple reviewers; minimal challenge ramp
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Sustained aggression from enemy AI; increased enemy density and behavior complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Level 5 (The Final Shell Shock) - climactic confrontation with Super Shredder; culmination of learned mechanics
notable_spikes: Level 4 (The Gauntlet) - title implies sustained gauntlet challenge

notable_level_patterns.description: Level 1 (New York City) teaches core dash/throw/melee mechanics against standard street enemies; Leatherhead boss provides first pattern-learning challenge
Level 2 introduces new setting (ghost ship) with implied environmental hazards; maintains combat systems while shifting spatial context
Level 4 named 'The Gauntlet' implies sustained challenge sequence; Level 5 named 'The Final Shell Shock' promises climactic endgame
notable_level_patterns.example_level: The Gauntlet / The Final Shell Shock

data_quality_notes: T2 tier qualitative extraction. Wikipedia provided level structure, boss progression, gameplay mechanics, and difficulty feedback from contemporary reviews. TCRF source was inaccessible due to content restriction notice. Specific timing data, detailed spatial maps, and enemy placement patterns not available in sources.
